ogTitle: Cookamidgera vs Cooks Gap: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cookamidgera and Cooks Gap.
heading: Cookamidgera vs Cooks Gap — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cookamidgera, New South Wales and Cooks Gap, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cookamidgera has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Cooks Gap (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cookamidgera or Cooks Gap safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cookamidgera has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Cooks Gap scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cookamidgera scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Cooks Gap scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cookamidgera or Cooks Gap?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cookamidgera has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
